title: amellus tridactylus subsp. arenarius
intro: Amellus tridactylus subsp. arenarius, also known as sea daphne, is a shrubby perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of sea daphne include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against various pathogens. Sea daphne can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
